prompt: What does your company ship and how do launches reach legal? Cover: what the product is (consumer / B2B / both, any regulated sector), who uses it, and the launch path to your desk — tracker (Jira / Linear / Asana / informal), lead time you usually get, and whether legal sign-off is a formal gate or advisory.

Which product-risk regimes fire at your company, and where do you draw the P0-vs-FYI line? Cover the axes that touch your launches — privacy / data, AI governance, children or minors, financial / fintech, health, IP, marketing-claims substantiation — and give one concrete example of something you blocked and one example of something that looked scary but you shipped.
